goal: Define the enforceable platform and regression lifecycle contract.
description: Scope: docs/plan/hook-enforcement/Requirements.md. Context: the user requires a Hook layer that checks platform before Node execution and runs accurate regression afterward. Target: define REQ-001 through REQ-007, users, workflow, constraints, scope, non-goals, and final acceptance. Design Considerations: keep host lifecycle behavior separate from state-machine guarantees and preserve immutable history. Design Value: a precise contract prevents advisory prose from being mistaken for enforcement. Constraints & Risks: Hook availability differs by host and evidence must not expose local or backend runtime data.

acceptance_criteria.text: Requirements.md defines REQ-001 through REQ-007, users, workflow, scope, non-goals, constraints, and final acceptance.

goal: Ground Hook enforcement in repository behavior and current host protocols.
description: Scope: docs/plan/hook-enforcement/Evidence.md and inspected workflow/installer code. Context: requirements need an evidence-backed enforcement boundary. Target: map current platform/completion gaps and official Codex, Cursor, and Claude Code Hook behavior to bounded design deductions. Design Considerations: use primary host documentation and current repository symbols; avoid retaining environment-specific observations. Design Value: evidence distinguishes reliable state-machine controls from host lifecycle conveniences. Constraints & Risks: external Hook protocols can change and source citations must remain direct.

acceptance_criteria.text: Evidence.md maps repository gaps and official host Hook behavior to the architecture decisions without private runtime data.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Repository and official host evidence mapped to bounded decisions.

goal: Map every Hook enforcement requirement to focused and final proof.
description: Scope: docs/plan/hook-enforcement/Validation.md. Context: the requirements and evidence define a cross-host correctness boundary. Target: map REQ-001 through REQ-007 to focused unit/CLI checks and one final full regression. Design Considerations: test host protocols with deterministic stdin/stdout fixtures and test installation in isolated temporary homes. Design Value: the matrix prevents host configuration success from masking workflow-state failure. Constraints & Risks: implementation Nodes must not run the complete suite.

acceptance_criteria.text: Validation.md maps every requirement to focused implementation proof and one final full-regression proof.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Focused and final proof mapped for all requirements.

commit.message: docs: add Hook enforcement validation matrix

goal: Fix the workflow, Hook, configuration, and delivery boundaries before implementation.
description: Scope: docs/plan/hook-enforcement/Architecture.md. Context: host protocols differ but platform/regression semantics must remain singular. Target: define module ownership, dependency direction, Node regression contract, Hook decisions, configuration merge contract, patterns, complexity, and risks. Design Considerations: use a functional core with thin protocol adapters and deterministic content-addressed receipts. Design Value: explicit boundaries prevent manifest_tool.py, install.py, and host translation from accumulating unrelated responsibilities. Constraints & Risks: preserve existing installer resolution and terminal Node history.

acceptance_criteria.text: Architecture.md defines module ownership, dependencies, contracts, deliberate patterns, complexity, and risk controls.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Module boundaries, contracts, patterns, and controls fixed before implementation.

goal: Enforce platform eligibility and machine-readable regression receipts in the workflow domain.
description: Scope: Closure: module - workflow-domain platform and regression contract; scripts/manifest_tool.py, tests/test_manifest_tool_cli.py, and tests/test_manifest_tool_state_machine.py. Context: start and completion currently rely on advisory prose and checked booleans. Target: add the Node contract, platform gate, regression runner, freshness receipt, completion fallback, schema/CLI support, and focused tests. Design Considerations: sorted path hashing, pre/post command fingerprints, bounded sequential subprocesses, atomic state writes, and immutable completed history. Design Value: one domain implementation guarantees correctness even when host Hooks are absent. Constraints & Risks: this bootstrap Node starts under the pre-Hook state machine, then must declare and pass its new focused contract before completion; never persist captured command output.

acceptance_criteria.text: Platform, schema, start, regress, receipt freshness, completion fallback, and final-validation ordering tests pass.
A failing regression records no receipt or checked criterion and exposes no captured output.

commit.message: feat: enforce Node platform and regression contracts

goal: Expose deterministic active-node discovery to lifecycle adapters.
description: Scope: Closure: module - workflow-domain active-node discovery API; scripts/manifest_tool.py and tests/test_manifest_tool_state_machine.py. Context: the architecture assigns workspace and active-node discovery to the workflow domain, while the Hook adapter must not duplicate state traversal. Target: discover nearest structural Better Plan workspaces from a project or nested working directory and return every in-progress Node location without guessing among them. Design Considerations: stop ascending at the nearest repository root, deduplicate manifests by resolved path, preserve stable ordering, and reuse existing structural discovery/load primitives. Design Value: one public query keeps host adapters thin and makes ambiguity explicit. Constraints & Risks: read-only discovery must skip unrelated dependency/build trees and must not change Node state.

acceptance_criteria.text: Focused state-machine tests prove project-root and nested-root discovery, stable ordering, deduplication, and multiple active Node reporting.

commit.message: feat: expose active Better Plan node discovery

goal: Translate Codex, Cursor, and Claude Code lifecycle events into the workflow domain.
description: Scope: Closure: module - cross-host Stop Hook protocol runtime; scripts/hook_tool.py and tests/test_hook_tool.py. Context: the workflow domain exposes singular platform and regression operations. Target: read host Stop JSON, discover one active Node, recheck platform compatibility, run stop-time regression, and serialize the host continuation response. Design Considerations: pure response strategies, exact-one active selection, no session persistence, no raw test output, and bounded error messages. Design Value: one thin adapter prevents three separate workflow implementations. Constraints & Risks: no workspace or ambiguous state is a no-op.

acceptance_criteria.text: Codex, Cursor, and Claude fixtures prove platform deny, stop continuation, successful receipt, ambiguity, and no-workspace behavior.
A failing command's sentinel output is absent from Hook stdout, state, and evidence.

commit.message: feat: add cross-host Better Plan Hook runtime

goal: Install and manage host Hook configurations without disturbing user entries.
description: Scope: Closure: module - Hook configuration and installer delivery; scripts/hook_config.py, scripts/install.py, and tests/test_install_tool.py. Context: the Hook runtime must be invoked by each supported host from its installed skill path. Target: add idempotent Codex/Cursor/Claude configuration merges, doctor checks, scoped uninstall, atomic writes, and focused installer tests. Design Considerations: stable command ownership marker, preservation of unknown fields/handlers, resolved per-agent script paths, and current shared/native selection. Design Value: declarative configuration ownership makes updates and removal safe. Constraints & Risks: do not overwrite unrelated settings or create duplicate handlers.

acceptance_criteria.text: Install/update create exactly one Stop handler for each supported host and doctor verifies the resolved runtime script.
Uninstall removes only Better Plan handlers while preserving unrelated configuration and repeated install is idempotent.

commit.message: feat: manage Better Plan Hook installation

goal: Document the enforced Hook lifecycle and Node regression contract.
description: Scope: Closure: capability - operator-facing Hook and regression workflow; SKILL.md, references/state-files.md, and README.md. Context: agents and users need the exact commands, contract fields, host coverage, trust behavior, and fallback semantics. Target: make pre-start platform checks, stop-time regression, complete fallback, installation, diagnosis, and uninstall discoverable. Design Considerations: distinguish hard state-machine guarantees from host-dependent lifecycle execution and keep examples repository-relative and privacy-safe. Design Value: clear operational guidance prevents bypass through obsolete manual steps. Constraints & Risks: do not claim that host Hooks replace native permissions or run when disabled.

acceptance_criteria.text: Skill, schema reference, and README consistently document the enforced lifecycle, contract, commands, host limitations, and privacy behavior.

commit.message: docs: explain Better Plan Hook enforcement

goal: Prove the Hook layer and manifest fallback enforce every requirement end to end.
description: Scope: full automated suite, installed-tree structural checks in isolated homes, workspace validation, requirement traceability, and one-time current-implementation search. Context: every focused implementation closure is complete. Target: prove REQ-001 through REQ-007 once using Validation.md. Design Considerations: run the complete suite exactly once and retain only redacted command-success evidence. Design Value: end-to-end proof verifies both host adapters and the host-independent hard boundary. Constraints & Risks: optional host executables may be unavailable; structural adapter proof remains distinct from live host execution.

acceptance_criteria.text: The complete automated regression suite passes once after all focused implementation checks.
Workspace validation, source checking, requirement labels, and one-current-implementation search pass.

commit.message: test: verify Hook enforcement end to end

commands: python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; forbidden = [p for p in Path(\"scripts\").iterdir() if p.name.startswith((\"manifest_tool_\", \"hook_tool_\", \"hook_config_\"))]; raise SystemExit(1 if forbidden else 0)"
